title: Diagnostic
description: Critical CSS Extraction Engine — Diagnostic wire schema (M5 crit-3, docs/design/1000-Diagnostics-Overview.md / docs/implementation/002-Milestones.md §M5). The machine-readable contract an editor extension / language server consumes to render inline diagnostics: one JSON object per `Diagnostic` the engine emits (packages/shared/src/dtos/diagnostic.ts). Mirrors that TypeScript interface field-for-field; kept from drifting by test/diagnostic-schema.test.ts, which validates every diagnostic the engine can actually construct (every ExtractionError subclass's toDiagnostic() output, plus every literal Diagnostic emitted by a pipeline stage) against this file, and fails if a `code` is emitted that is not enumerated here.

message.description: Human-readable message. Not machine-matched — use `code` for programmatic branching.

context.description: Structured, JSON-serializable context (route, viewport, elapsed ms, …). Shape varies per `code`; not enumerated here — an editor extension should treat it as opaque key/value data to display, not to parse structurally.

severity.description: docs/design/1000-Diagnostics-Overview.md — maps directly to LSP DiagnosticSeverity (error=1, warning=2, information=3) an editor extension would use: error, warning, info in that priority order.

url.description: URL of the artifact (page, stylesheet) this diagnostic refers to. null for inline/synthetic sources.

code.description: Stable, machine-readable diagnostic code. The complete catalog of codes the engine can currently emit as a structured `Diagnostic` (packages/shared's `Diagnostic` type) is enumerated below — this is the code catalog crit-3 requires. Codes are grouped by originating error class / pipeline stage for readability; the enum itself is flat (an editor extension does not need to know which stage emitted a code, only how to react to it).
